name: Physics.Body

unions.name: Behavior
unions.comment: Bodies may have static or dynamic behavior.

aliases.name: Body
aliases.comment:
Represents a physical body containing
user defined data, like a WebGL mesh.

By default bodies don’t move. To change this,
use [setBehavior](#setBehavior).

All bodies start out centered on the origin,
use [moveTo](#moveTo) to set the position.

The supported bodies are:

  - [block](#block),
  - [plane](#plane),
  - [sphere](#sphere),
  - [particle](#particle).

For complex bodies check [compound](#compound).

values.name: block
values.comment:
A block is created from elm-geometry [Block3d](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/ianmackenzie/elm-geometry/latest/Block3d).
To create a 1x1x1 cube, centered at the origin of
the body, call this:

    cubeBody =
        block
            (Block3d.centeredOn
                Frame3d.origin
                ( meters 1, meters 1, meters 1 )
            )
            data
values.type: Block3d.Block3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: compound
values.comment:
Make a compound body from a list of [shapes](Physics-Shape#Shape).

For example, the [sphere](#sphere) from above can be defined like this:

    sphere radius data =
        compound
            [ Shape.sphere
                (Sphere3d.atOrigin (meters 1))
            ]
            data

We only support [rigid bodies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rigid_body).

values.name: dynamic
values.comment:
Dynamic bodies move and react to forces and collide with
other dynamic and static bodies.

values.name: getData
values.comment: Get user-defined data.

values.name: getFrame3d
values.comment:
Get the position and orientation of the body in the world
as [Frame3d](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/ianmackenzie/elm-geometry/latest/Frame3d).

This is useful to transform points and directions between
world and body coordinates.
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> Frame3d.Frame3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.WorldCoordinates { defines : Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates }

values.name: moveTo
values.comment:
Set the position of the body in the world,
e.g. to raise a body 5 meters above the origin:

    movedBody =
        body
            |> moveTo (Point3d.meters 0 0 5)
values.type: Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.WorldCoordinates -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: originPoint
values.comment: Get the origin point of a body in the world

values.name: particle
values.comment:
A particle is an abstract point that doesn’t have dimensions.
Particles don’t collide with each other.

values.name: plane
values.comment:
A plane with the normal that points
in the direction of the z axis.

A plane is collidable in the direction of the normal.
Planes don’t collide with other planes and are always static.

values.name: rotateAround
values.comment:
Rotate the body in the world around axis,
e.g. to rotate a body 45 degrees around Z axis:

    movedBody =
        body
            |> rotateAround Axis3d.z (Angle.degrees 45)
values.type: Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.WorldCoordinates -> Angle.Angle -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: setBehavior
values.comment:
Change the behavior, e.g. to make a body dynamic:

    dynamicBody =
        staticBody
            |> setBehavior (dynamic (Mass.kilograms 5))

values.name: setData
values.comment: Set user-defined data.

values.name: setMaterial
values.comment: Set the [material](Physics-Material) to controll friction and bounciness.

values.name: sphere
values.comment:
A sphere is created from elm-geometry [Sphere3d](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/ianmackenzie/elm-geometry/latest/Sphere3d).

To create a 1 meter radius sphere, that is centered
at the origin of the body, call this:

    sphereBody =
        sphere
            (Sphere3d.atOrigin (meters 1))
            data
values.type: Sphere3d.Sphere3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: static
values.comment: Static bodies don’t move and only collide with dynamic bodies.

values.name: translateBy
values.comment:
Move the body in the world relative to its current position,
e.g. to translate a body down by 5 meters:

    translatedBody =
        body
            |> translateBy (Vector3d.meters 0 0 -5)
values.type: Vector3d.Vector3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.WorldCoordinates -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

aliases.name: Constraint
aliases.comment:
Constraint allows to limit the freedom of movement
of two bodies with relation to each other.

values.name: distance
values.comment:
Keep the centers of two bodies at the constant distance
from each other.

values.name: hinge
values.comment:
Keep two bodies connected with each other and limit the freedom of rotation.
Useful for e.g. connecting a window to a window frame, or to connect a wheel to a car.
values.type: Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> Physics.Constraint.Constraint

values.name: pointToPoint
values.comment:
Connect a point on the first body with a point on the second body.
Points are defined within the bodies’ local coordinate systems.
values.type: Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> Physics.Constraint.Constraint

name: Physics.Coordinates
comment:
These type parameters indicate coordinate systems.

@docs WorldCoordinates, BodyCoordinates

name: Physics.Debug

aliases.name: FaceNormal
aliases.comment:
A face normal consists of a normal vector for a face
and a reference point on the face.

These are both expressed within the local body coordinate system.
aliases.type: { normal : Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, point : Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates }

aliases.name: UniqueEdge
aliases.comment:
A unique edge consists of a unit direction vector
that runs parallel to an edge of a face.

A vertex point of the body is also provided for context.

These are both expressed within the local body coordinate system.
aliases.type: { direction : Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, point : Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates }

values.name: getCenterOfMass
values.comment: Get the center of mass of the body.

values.name: getContacts
values.comment: Get the contact points in the world.

values.name: getFaceNormals
values.comment: Get the face normals of the body.

values.name: getUniqueEdges
values.comment: Get the unique edges of the body.

aliases.name: Material
aliases.comment:
Materials allow to control friction and bounciness.
You can change materials using [Body.setMaterial](Physics-Body#setMaterial).

values.name: custom
values.comment:
Creates a custom material, e.g.

    slippery =
        custom { friction = 0, bounciness = 0 }

    bouncy =
        custom { friction = 0.3, bounciness = 0.9 }

When two materials collide, their properties are averaged
and clamped between 0 and 1.

values.name: default

aliases.name: Shape
aliases.comment:
Shapes are only needed for creating [compound](Physics-Body#compound) bodies.

If you need a body with a single shape, use the corresponding functions
from the [Physics.Body](Physics-Body) module.

The only supported shapes are:

  - [block](#block),
  - [sphere](#sphere).

Shapes are defined in the body coordinate system.

name: Physics.World

aliases.name: RaycastResult
aliases.comment:
The Raycast result includes the intersected body,
intersection point and normal vector on the face,
expressed within the local body coordinate system.

Use the `Frame3d` from [Body.getFrame3d](Physics-Body#getFrame3d)
to transform the result into world coordinates.
aliases.type: { body : Physics.Body.Body data, point : Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, normal : Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates }

aliases.name: World
aliases.comment: Physical world is our abstract playground for physical simulations.

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a body to the world.

    worldWithFloor =
        add planeBody world

Check the [Physics.Body](Physics-Body) module for possible bodies.
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: constrain
values.comment:
Configure constraints between pairs of bodies. Constraints allow to limit the
freedom of movement of two bodies with relation to each other.

Check the [Physics.Constraint](Physics-Constraint) module for possible constraints.

    worldWithACar : World { part : String }
    worldWithACar =
        constrain
            (\b1 b2 ->
                case
                    ( (Body.getData b1).part
                    , (Body.getData b2).part
                    )
                of
                    ( "wheel1", "base" ) ->
                        [ hingeConstraint1 ]

                    ( "wheel2", "base" ) ->
                        [ hingeConstraint2 ]

                    ( "wheel3", "base" ) ->
                        [ hingeConstraint3 ]

                    ( "wheel4", "base" ) ->
                        [ hingeConstraint4 ]

                    _ ->
                        []
            )
            worldWithCarParts

Note that this example only works for a single car, otherwise it would
connect wheels of one car with the base of another another. You might want
to use `constrainIf` to apply constraints on a subset of bodies.

    constrain =
        constrainIf (always True)
values.type: (Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data -> List.List Physics.Constraint.Constraint) -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: constrainIf
values.comment:
Configure constraints for a subset of bodies that satisfy the test.

For the above example we can tag each part of a car with the `carId`,
and preselect parts of a single car with:

    constrainCar carId =
        constrainIf
            (\body ->
                (Body.getData body).carId == carId
            )
values.type: (Physics.Body.Body data -> Basics.Bool) -> (Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data -> List.List Physics.Constraint.Constraint) -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: empty
values.comment: Let be the new world!

values.name: getBodies
values.comment:
Get all bodies from the world.

Use this to convert bodies into visual representation,
e.g. WebGL entities.

values.name: keepIf
values.comment: Keep bodies that satisfy the test.
values.type: (Physics.Body.Body data -> Basics.Bool) -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: raycast
values.comment:
Find the closest intersection of a ray against
all the bodies in the world. Except for particles,
because they have no size.
values.type: Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.WorldCoordinates -> Physics.World.World data -> Maybe.Maybe (Physics.World.RaycastResult data)

values.name: setGravity
values.comment:
Set the [standard gravity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_gravity) and its direction, e.g.:

    planetEarth =
        setGravity
            (Acceleration.metersPerSecondSquared 9.80665)
            Direction3d.negativeZ
            world
values.type: Acceleration.Acceleration -> Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.Coordinates.WorldCoordinates -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: simulate
values.comment:
Simulate the world, given the number of seconds since the last frame.

To animate, call this on a message from [onAnimationFrameDelta](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/browser/latest/Browser-Events#onAnimationFrameDelta):

    simulatedWorld =
        World.simulate (Duration.seconds (1 / 60)) world

Make sure to pass a short duration, such that bodies do not travel
through each other during the single frame. This depends on minimum body
size and maximum velocity.

values.name: update
values.comment: Apply a function to every body in the world.
values.type: (Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data) -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data
